title: Goroutines, Channels, Select, Ownership, and Pipelines · Contract Triage
summary: A report pipeline must process bounded work concurrently, preserve result ownership, close channels correctly, and terminate every goroutine. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a bounded concurrent report pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging,…
objectives: Start goroutines only with named ownership, inputs, outputs, stop conditions, panic boundary, and completion evidence.
Use unbuffered, buffered, and directional channels while assigning close authority to the sender that knows no values remain.
Distinguish open, closed, empty, nil, and buffered channel states and interpret two-result receives and range termination.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Pipeline Fanout Fanin
steps.instruction:
Order the Pipeline Fanout Fanin evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Pipeline Fanout Fanin.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Pipeline Fanout Fanin assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Pipeline Fanout Fanin, keep Pipeline Fanout Fanin connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: Pipeline 3 starts 5 workers, has a two-slot buffer, one early consumer exit, two simultaneously ready select cases, and one unclosed output. Trace ownership, blocking, close authority, result order, and every goroutine's stop path. Competency probe: Build bounded fan-out and fan-in stages with one ownership model, ordered-result policy, error propagation, and leak checks.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Pipeline Fanout Fanin has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.
